Explain the concept of virtual memory in operating systems, including how page tables and page faults work, and why this abstraction is useful for modern applications running on hardware with limited physical RAM. Provide examples to illustrate.

Virtual memory is a feature of most modern operating systems that allows a computer to compensate for insufficient physical memory by temporarily transferring pages (blocks of data) from the physical memory to the hard disk (or SSD). This is done automatically
